banner.title: Blaan Beadwork
banner.subtitle: Crafting Heritage through Traditional Jewelry & Adornments

process.title: The Art of Adornment
process.subtitle: Our students master the intricate patterns inspired by Blaan Tabih designs.

steps.title: Selection of Kasì (Beads)
steps.description: We use vibrant seed beads in the five traditional colors: Black, White, Red, Yellow, and Blue. These form the foundation of all Blaan-inspired jewelry.

steps.title: Pattern Mastery
steps.description: Students study ancestral Tabih weaving motifs, translating those geometric symbols into beadwork patterns for necklaces, bracelets, and earrings.

steps.title: Assembly & Jewelry Craft
steps.description: Combining beadwork with brass-linked accents and Takmon (shell) sequins to create modern jewelry that carries the soul of the Blaan tribe.

gallery.title: Student Beadwork Gallery
gallery.subtitle: Initial projects from our Cultural Heritage Learning Center.
